identity.name: Mike Garza
identity.title: Owner / Shop Manager

pbContactData.summary: 18 years in precision CNC machining. Started at Boeing as a machinist, opened Garza Precision in 2014 with savings and an SBA loan. Runs a 5-machine shop specializing in aerospace brackets and hydraulic manifolds. Active NTMA chapter member.

identity.fitReason: 5-machine CNC shop, aerospace + hydraulic work, high air demand, current compressor undersized and broken

rapport_starters: NTMA member — ask about the last chapter meeting in Mesa
Aerospace brackets = tight tolerances, mention clean dry air
18 yrs machining, started at Boeing — respect the craft, skip the sales pitch
Started his own shop in 2014 with an SBA loan — self-made, hates corporate fluff
Hydraulic manifolds = oily environment, filtration matters
intel_nuggets: Running 5 CNC machines (Haas VF-2 x3, Mazak QTN-200 x2) — needs ~40 CFM at 125 PSI.
Current piston compressor BROKE YESTERDAY — renting from Sunbelt at $200/day. Urgent buyer.
AS9100 aerospace environment — clean dry air is a compliance requirement, not a nice-to-have.
Shop is in Mesa, AZ (east Phoenix metro) — 15 min from Scottsdale HQ.
Owner-operator with 15 employees, $2.4M revenue. He makes the decisions. No committee.
Has 460V 3-phase power — no electrical upgrade needed for any of our units.
rapport.opening_line: Hey Mike, I saw you guys do aerospace bracket work — I had a question about how you handle air quality for those tight tolerance jobs.
rapport.adapted_script: His compressor is DEAD. He's bleeding $200/day on a rental. Lead with urgency — 'I heard you might be dealing with a compressor issue.' Then pivot to sizing: ask about his machines (he'll tell you — Haas VF-2s and Mazak QTN-200s). LiveAnalysis will calculate his air demand automatically. When it shows ~40 CFM at 125 PSI, recommend the JRS-10E (42 CFM, 460V, $9,495). Mention the rotary screw eliminates moisture and short-cycling. For AS9100 work, upsell the desiccant dryer (JDD-40) — not the refrigerated one. If he asks about lead time or installation, those are buying signals.
watch_outs: He built this shop himself — respect independence, don't be pushy
Compressor just broke — he's frustrated but motivated. Don't waste his time.
AS9100 compliance is sensitive — ask what standard they run, don't assume
Don't oversell — he knows his shop, he knows CFM and PSI. Talk peer-to-peer.
product_reference: JRS-10E — 10HP, 42 CFM @ 125 PSI, 460V/3ph, $9,495. Enclosed rotary screw.
JDD-40 — Desiccant dryer, -60°F dewpoint, wall mount, $7,495. For AS9100.
JRD-40 — Refrigerated dryer, 38°F dewpoint, 115V, $2,495. General shop use.
JCF-70 — Coalescing filter, $349. Oil-free air for aerospace.
JPF-70 — Particulate pre-filter, $399. Upstream of dryer.

interactions.summary: Opened 'Emergency compressor replacement' email 5x in 2 hours — urgent interest
Clicked link to compressor sizing calculator
Opened VSD rotary screw compressor email 3x — strong interest

icpScore.fit_reason: 5-machine CNC shop, aerospace + hydraulic verticals, high air demand, Mesa AZ metro, owner-operator decision maker, compressor just failed
